mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during periods of rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and investment demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals strengthening supply-side capacity utilization, which boosts corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days, but only during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration predicts negative or flat 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% year-over-year, or the signal Rank IC turns negative in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in economic strength.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no correlation with 5-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation spikes above 0.4% force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, which depresses long-duration equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in greater than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4%, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions with t-test p-value greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE predicts negative 20-day returns, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward regression tests with coefficient p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in real personal spending signals weakening consumer demand that immediately pressures corporate revenue forecasts, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days, particularly when credit conditions are already tight.
failure_condition: RSAFS deceleration fails to predict negative 5-day returns when credit spreads are low, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% in tight credit regimes over a 3-year rolling window.
